content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "ui-design-system",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 68,
    "domain_depth": 55,
    "data_richness": 50,
    "interaction_quality": 60,
    "ai_integration": 30,
    "mobile_first": 45,
    "performance": 62,
    "typescript_quality": 58,
    "error_handling": 52,
    "realtime": 40,
    "accessibility": 38,
    "security": 55,
    "differentiation": 45,
    "enterprise_readiness": 50
  },
  "overall_score": 51,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "ContextMenu items lack ARIA roles and keyboard navigation",
      "file": "src/components/ContextMenu.tsx",
      "description": "The context menu and toast system lack proper ARIA roles (menu, menuitem), keyboard support (Arrow keys, Enter, Escape to close menu), and focus management. This fails WCAG 2.1 AA and the global acceptance criteria requiring axe-core zero critical violations and full keyboard navigability.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, make these specific changes: In any context menu rendering component/function, add role='menu' to the menu container and role='menuitem' to each menu item. Add tabIndex={0} to menu items. Add onKeyDown handlers: ArrowDown moves focus to next item, ArrowUp to previous, Enter/Space triggers the item action, Escape closes the menu. Wrap the menu container in a FocusTrap or manually manage focus so that when the menu opens, focus moves to the first item, and when it closes, focus returns to the trigger element. BEFORE: Menu items are plain divs/buttons with no ARIA roles, no keyboard arrow navigation, and no focus trapping. AFTER: Menu has role='menu', items have role='menuitem' with tabIndex, arrow key navigation cycles through items, Escape closes menu and returns focus to trigger. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "Breadcrumbs truncation has syntax error and no touch target sizing",
      "file": "src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx",
      "description": "The Breadcrumbs component has a raw template literal artifact (`\\n\\n`) visible in the source, and breadcrumb buttons use no minimum height/width for touch targets. The global acceptance criteria require 44x44px minimum touch targets per Apple HIG and usability at 375px width.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx, make these specific changes: 1) In the crumbButtonStyle object, add minHeight: '44px' and minWidth: '44px' to ensure touch targets meet the 44x44px Apple HIG minimum. 2) Fix the FolderBreadcrumbs component's nav style block which appears to have a truncated/malformed template literal at the end of the file (the code cuts off mid-style). Ensure the nav element's style object is properly closed with valid CSS-in-JS properties including flexWrap: 'wrap' for mobile overflow. 3) Add aria-current='page' to the last breadcrumb item to indicate the current location. BEFORE: Breadcrumb buttons have no minimum touch target size (padding only provides ~28px height), file appears truncated with incomplete JSX. AFTER: All breadcrumb buttons meet 44x44px minimum, nav renders correctly on mobile with wrapping, last crumb has aria-current='page'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-003",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "Toast auto-dismiss has no pause-on-hover and no undo action support",
      "file": "src/components/ContextMenu.tsx",
      "description": "Toasts auto-dismiss after 5 seconds with no way to pause the timer on hover or focus. The global acceptance criteria require bulk actions to show undo for 8 seconds. Error toasts should persist until dismissed. The current implementation treats all severities identically with a fixed 5s timeout.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ToastEntry component, make these specific changes: 1) Add onMouseEnter and onMouseLeave handlers that pause and resume the auto-dismiss timer respectively. Use a ref to track remaining time. 2) Change the timeout duration based on severity: 'error' toasts should NOT auto-dismiss (user must click dismiss). 'success' and 'info' toasts dismiss after 5 seconds. 'warning' toasts dismiss after 8 seconds. 3) Add an optional 'action' prop to ToastItem interface with { label: string; onClick: () => void } for undo support, and render it as a button inside the toast when provided. BEFORE: All toasts auto-dismiss after exactly 5 seconds regardless of severity, no pause on hover, no action/undo button support. AFTER: Error toasts persist until dismissed, warning toasts last 8s, hovering pauses the timer, optional undo action button renders inside the toast. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "visual_polish",
      "title": "Theme tokens missing skeleton loader animation and consistent loading states",
      "file": "src/styles/theme.ts",
      "description": "The design standards mandate skeleton loaders with pulse animation (opacity 0.3 to 0.7, 1.5s cycle) matching exact content shapes. The global acceptance criteria require skeleton loaders within 100ms of navigation. No skeleton animation keyframes or skeleton-specific tokens exist in the theme file, forcing each component to implement its own inconsistent loading state.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/styles/theme.ts, make these specific changes: Add a new 'skeleton' section to the exported theme tokens containing: 1) skeleton.baseColor set to '#E5E7EB' (matching the border color token). 2) skeleton.highlightColor set to '#F3F4F6'. 3) skeleton.animation as a string value 'pulse 1.5s ease-in-out infinite' for use in CSS animation property. 4) skeleton.borderRadius set to borderRadius.md for consistent rounded corners. 5) Also add a 'keyframes' export object with a 'pulse' key containing the string '@keyframes pulse { 0%, 100% { opacity: 0.3; } 50% { opacity: 0.7; } }' so components can inject it into style tags or styled-components. BEFORE: No skeleton-related tokens exist in theme.ts; components must hardcode skeleton colors and animations independently. AFTER: Theme exports skeleton.baseColor, skeleton.highlightColor, skeleton.animation, skeleton.borderRadius, and keyframes.pulse for consistent skeleton loaders across all pages. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "typescript_quality",
      "title": "Theme color tokens lack TypeScript const assertion and type exports",
      "file": "src/styles/theme.ts",
      "description": "The theme file exports plain objects without 'as const' assertions, meaning all color values are typed as 'string' rather than their literal values. Components cannot get autocomplete on specific color values, and there are no exported TypeScript types for color keys, spacing keys, or typography variants. This makes it easy to use invalid token names without compiler errors.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/styles/theme.ts, make these specific changes: 1) Add 'as const' assertion to the colors, spacing, typography, borderRadius, shadows, zIndex, and transitions export objects (e.g., 'export const colors = { ... } as const'). 2) Export derived types: 'export type ColorToken = keyof typeof colors', 'export type SpacingToken = keyof typeof spacing', 'export type ShadowToken = keyof typeof shadows'. 3) Add a type-safe helper function: 'export function themeColor(token: ColorToken): string { return colors[token]; }' that provides autocomplete and compile-time validation when accessing theme colors. BEFORE: All theme exports are plain objects typed as Record<string, string>, no exported types for token keys, no compile-time validation when using token names. AFTER: All theme objects use 'as const' for literal types, ColorToken/SpacingToken/ShadowToken types are exported, themeColor helper provides type-safe access. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's UI uses a mature design system (Blueprint) with full ARIA compliance, consistent skeleton loaders, and comprehensive keyboard navigation. Fieldwire excels at mobile touch targets with 48px minimum across their field interface. SiteSync's design system has solid color tokens and spacing foundations but lacks the accessibility rigor, skeleton loader infrastructure, and mobile touch target enforcement that enterprise customers expect. The toast system and context menu need WCAG compliance work before passing any enterprise accessibility audit.",
  "summary": "The UI Design System module provides a reasonable foundation with theme tokens for colors, spacing, typography, and transitions, but falls short of the 'Google level polish' vision in several critical areas. The most impactful issues are: (1) missing ARIA roles and keyboard navigation on interactive components like context menus, which blocks WCAG 2.1 AA compliance required by global acceptance criteria; (2) breadcrumb touch targets below the 44px minimum, with what appears to be a truncated source file; (3) toast notifications that lack severity-aware auto-dismiss behavior and undo action support required by the acceptance criteria; (4) no centralized skeleton loader tokens despite skeleton loaders being mandated on every page; and (5) theme objects lacking TypeScript const assertions, preventing compile-time validation of token usage. The fix rate from Cycle 1 was only 8%, so these prompts are deliberately scoped to single files with precise before/after descriptions to maximize successful application."
}
```
